From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Mexican long-distance runner

In this [Spanish name](/wiki/Spanish_name "Spanish name"), the first or paternal [surname](/wiki/Surname "Surname") is  *Espinoza* and the second or maternal family name is  *Pérez*.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/e/e4/28af1.jpg)](/wiki/File:28af1.jpg)

Espinosa in action

**Andrés Espinoza Pérez** (born 4 February 1963) is a Mexican former long-distance runner who specialized in [marathon races](/wiki/Marathon_race "Marathon race").

Espinosa finished ninth at the [1995 World Championships](/wiki/1995_World_Championships_in_Athletics "1995 World Championships in Athletics") in 2:16:44 hours. He won the 1993 [New York Marathon](/wiki/New_York_Marathon "New York Marathon") and achieved his personal best time of 2:07:19 hours at the 1994 [Boston Marathon](/wiki/Boston_Marathon "Boston Marathon"). He set the world master (+40) record for the marathon in Berlin Marathon 2003 (2:08:46), which lasted nearly a dozen years until it was broken by 2 seconds in 2015 by Evergreen Kenneth Mungara. He also won the [Lisbon Half Marathon](/wiki/Lisbon_Half_Marathon "Lisbon Half Marathon") in 1994.[[1]](#cite_note-1)
